pysoniq.utils module¶

Utility functions

pysoniq.utils.bandpass(audio_filepath, lowcut, highcut, order=4)[source]¶

Apply a bandpass filter to an audio file and return the filtered audio array.

Parameters:

  • audio_file – Path to audio file

  • lowcut – Low frequency cutoff in Hz

  • highcut – High frequency cutoff in Hz

  • order – Filter order (higher = steeper rolloff)

Returns:

Filtered audio data (not a tuple)

Return type:

numpy.ndarray

pysoniq.utils.db_to_linear(db, ref=1.0)[source]¶

Convert decibels to linear magnitude

Parameters:

  • db – numpy array, values in dB

  • ref – float, reference value (default 1.0)

Returns:

linear magnitude values

pysoniq.utils.hz_to_mel(hz)[source]¶

Convert Hz to mel scale

Parameters:

hz – float or array, frequency in Hz

Returns:

float or array, frequency in mels

Return type:

mel

pysoniq.utils.int32_to_24bit_bytes(data_int32)[source]¶

Convert int32 array to 24-bit byte array

pysoniq.utils.linear_to_db(S, ref=1.0)[source]¶

Convert linear magnitude to decibels

Parameters:

  • S – numpy array, linear magnitude values

  • ref – float, reference value (default 1.0)

Returns:

dB values

pysoniq.utils.mel_to_hz(mel)[source]¶

Convert mel scale to Hz

Parameters:

mel – float or array, frequency in mels

Returns:

float or array, frequency in Hz

Return type:

hz

pysoniq.utils.normalize_audio(audio, target_level=-3.0)[source]¶

Normalize audio to target dB level

Parameters:

  • audio – numpy array

  • target_level – float, target dB level

Returns:

normalized audio

pysoniq.utils.resample_to_fixed_length(y, target_len, verbose=False)[source]¶

Resample a 1-D signal to a fixed sample count via linear interpolation.

Maps the native time axis onto target_len uniformly spaced points, independent of native duration. Preserves spectro-temporal shape while normalizing length; absolute duration is not preserved.

pysoniq.utils.to_int16(audio)[source]¶

Convert float audio to 16-bit PCM

Parameters:

audio – numpy array, float, -1 to 1

Returns:

int16 array

pysoniq.utils.to_pcm(data, target_bits=16)[source]¶

Convert float audio to PCM with auto bit depth detection

Parameters:

  • data – numpy array, float

  • target_bits – 8, 16, 24, or 32

Returns:

bytes per sample data_int: integer array

Return type:

sampwidth

pysoniq
